hero.subtitle: The official women's catchball association in the Netherlands
about.label: Who We Are
about.title: The Netherlands Catchball Association
about.text: The Netherlands Catchball Association (NCA) is the official governing body for women's catchball in the Netherlands. Founded in December 2019, we connect women through sport, competition, and community — building a league that grows stronger every season.
cb.label: The Sport
cb.title: What is Catchball?
cb.text: Catchball is a dynamic women's team sport combining volleyball and handball. Two teams of 6 compete across a net — passing, catching, no spiking needed. Accessible, competitive, and seriously fun.
cb.c1p: Simple rules make catchball perfect for beginners and experienced athletes alike.
cb.c2p: 6 players per side — built on communication, teamwork, and trust.
cb.c3p: No age limit, no prior experience needed. Just bring your energy!
cb.c4p: Make lasting friendships on and off the court.
cb.rules.label: The Rules
cb.rules.title: How to Play Catchball
cb.rules.text: Catchball is played on a standard volleyball court with a net. The game combines elements of volleyball and handball, but instead of spiking, players throw and catch the ball. It's designed to be inclusive — no prior sports experience needed.
cb.r1p: Played on a volleyball-sized court (18×9m) with a net at 2.15m height. Two teams of 6 players face each other across the net. Players rotate positions after winning a serve, just like in volleyball.
cb.r2p: The ball is served by throwing it over the net. The receiving team catches the ball and has up to 3 passes between teammates before throwing it back over the net. A player may hold the ball for a maximum of 3 seconds. No spiking or hitting — the ball must always be thrown and caught.
cb.r3p: A point is scored when the ball touches the ground on the opponent's side, or when the opposing team commits a fault (out of bounds, too many passes, holding too long). Rally scoring is used — a point is awarded on every play regardless of which team served.
cb.r4p: Sets are played to 25 points (win by 2). A match is typically best of 3 sets. If a deciding set is needed, it is played to 15 points (win by 2). Teams switch sides between sets.
cb.r5p: All you need is a catchball (similar to a small volleyball), a net, and a court. Players wear comfortable sportswear and indoor/outdoor court shoes. No special protective gear needed.
cb.r6p: Catchball originated in Israel, where it became one of the most popular women's sports. The sport has since spread to communities across Europe and beyond, including the Netherlands, where the NCA was founded in 2019.
join.label: Get Involved
join.title: Ready to Play?
join.text: Whether you're a seasoned athlete or picking up a ball for the first time, NCA welcomes you. Join our Facebook community to find a team near you or start a new one.
